summary: Historical research record for the old Modern Standby registry bundle. Microsoft says switching between Modern Standby and S3 is not a supported Windows setting change, so this bundle is no longer part of the live app surface.

setting.name: Modern Standby Power Model
setting.casual_explanation: Modern Standby is the low-power idle sleep model used on many newer systems. Older tweak lists tried to disable it with registry writes that were never supported as a Windows-side switch.
setting.why_people_change_it: People look for this when they want classic sleep behavior or when they suspect Modern Standby is involved in battery drain, wake, or sleep issues.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Microsoft says switching power models is not supported by changing a Windows setting. That leaves this bundle as research history, not a live tweak.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Modern Standby documentation states that switching between S3 and Modern Standby cannot be done by changing a setting and is not supported in Windows without a complete OS reinstall. The repo still keeps the old MSDisabled and ModernSleep\EnabledActions bundle as historical evidence because it appears in local traces and older tweak lists, but it is no longer exposed in the app.

allowed_values.label: Unsupported Windows-side switch
allowed_values.meaning: The official Microsoft guidance used in this review does not support switching between Modern Standby and S3 through a registry setting.

app_current_implementation.notes: The live app surface no longer exposes this unsupported power-model switch.

windows_defaults.label: Hardware and image dependent baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems whose shipped platform design supports either Modern Standby or S3

states.rationale: The shipped sleep model depends on platform support and image design, not on a validated post-install registry toggle.

windows_defaults.notes: This review follows Microsoft's guidance that switching power models is not a supported Windows setting change.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows shipped baseline
intended_for: Systems staying on their supported sleep model
avoid_for: None until a supported Microsoft transition path exists

states.rationale: Stay on the system's supported shipped sleep model.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to stay aligned with Microsoft's supported behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to use an undocumented registry path to force a power-model change.
tradeoffs: The system stays on its supported sleep model.
The current registry-based tweak remains documented but not validated.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: What is Modern Standby
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents Modern Standby and states that switching between S3 and Modern Standby is not supported in Windows without a complete OS reinstall.

evidence.title: Former app implementation
evidence.summary: The older app implementation wrote MSDisabled = 1 and ModernSleep\EnabledActions = 0 before the tweak was removed from the live surface.

evidence.title: nohuto power trace for MSDisabled
evidence.summary: Power.txt lists MSDisabled in the power values block, showing the same local registry family used by the current app implementation.

evidence.title: Repo power notes
evidence.summary: The repo tracks these registry values as power research, but this review did not promote them to a supported Microsoft transition mechanism.

decision.why: Microsoft's guidance does not support switching between Modern Standby and S3 through a Windows setting change. The bundle stays only as historical research.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Switching the power model is not supported in Windows without a complete OS re-install.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft documents that switching between S3 and Modern Standby is not supported through a Windows setting change, so the old registry bundle was retired from the app.
